research_question: After VSUMD60 passed confirmatory rounds 1 and 2 as a standalone Alpha158 atomic winner, can one restrained family seed that changes only the ranking eligibility guard improve tradability and cost robustness without giving back the confirmed standalone edge?

time_partition_plan.note: The frozen 2022-2025 test window remains excluded by construction through the trainval-only snapshot.

guard_contract.guard_definition: Keep the VSUMD60 score unchanged. Only rows that are already ranking_eligible_D0 and whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on the same signal_date may enter the ranking universe.
guard_contract.treatment_of_rows_below_guard: Remain in audit tables but must be excluded from ranking_state_daily eligibility.

constants_frozen.portfolio_extraction_rule: inherit current equal-weight standalone extraction from the confirmatory VSUMD60 reference unless explicitly superseded in a later seed round
constants_frozen.portfolio_refresh_rule: inherit current full daily refresh from the standalone confirmatory reference unless explicitly superseded in a later seed round

decision_rule.interpretation: The seed is allowed to trade a small amount of standalone edge only if it clearly improves tradability and cost robustness; otherwise the standalone VSUMD60 reference remains the promoted object.

design_constraints: Only the ranking eligibility guard may change in this seed round.
Do not reopen the failed Alpha158-to-v18 direct overlay path.
Do not add any second atomic signal.
Do not change TopK width, extraction geometry, refresh hysteresis, benchmark, or execution semantics in this round.
disallowed_changes: Do not change the VSUMD60 score field or ranking direction.
Do not add multi-signal blends or overlays.
Do not change the snapshot, contract, or validation window after registration.
Do not change the guard threshold or guard stage after registration.
Do not compare only to v18; the primary reference for this round must be the confirmatory standalone VSUMD60 winner.
notes: This prereg defines the first restrained family-seed transition from the confirmed standalone VSUMD60 winner.
The seed is intentionally minimal: keep the score fixed, add only a ranking liquidity guard, and judge it primarily against standalone VSUMD60 rather than against v18.
